title: Require MFA for Nonlocal Maintenance Sessions and Terminate When Complete
description: require multifactor authentication to establish nonlocal maintenance sessions via external network connections and terminate such connections when nonlocal maintenance is complete

checks.name: MFA is Used to Establish Nonlocal Maintenance Sessions via External Network Connections
checks.purpose: Enforcement of MFA for nonlocal maintenance sessions is an organizational policy and identity management concern dependent on the MFA solution in use and is not technically verifiable via automated check on Windows Client

checks.name: Nonlocal Maintenance Sessions are Terminated When Maintenance is Complete
checks.purpose: Termination of nonlocal maintenance sessions upon completion is an organizational process concern requiring manual verification or privileged access management tooling and is not technically verifiable via automated check on Windows Client

checks.name: MFA is Used to Establish Nonlocal Maintenance Sessions via External Network Connections
checks.purpose: Enforcement of MFA for nonlocal maintenance sessions is an organizational policy and identity management concern and is not technically verifiable via automated check on Windows Server

checks.name: Nonlocal Maintenance Sessions are Terminated When Maintenance is Complete
checks.purpose: Termination of nonlocal maintenance sessions upon completion is an organizational process concern and is not technically verifiable via automated check on Windows Server

checks.name: MFA is Used to Establish Nonlocal Maintenance Sessions via External Network Connections
checks.purpose: Enforcement of MFA for nonlocal maintenance sessions is an organizational policy and identity management concern and is not technically verifiable via automated check on Linux

checks.name: Nonlocal Maintenance Sessions are Terminated When Maintenance is Complete
checks.purpose: Termination of nonlocal maintenance sessions upon completion is an organizational process concern and is not technically verifiable via automated check on Linux

checks.name: MFA is Used to Establish Nonlocal Maintenance Sessions via External Network Connections
checks.purpose: Enforcement of MFA for nonlocal maintenance sessions is an organizational policy and identity management concern and is not technically verifiable via automated check on Debian

checks.name: Nonlocal Maintenance Sessions are Terminated When Maintenance is Complete
checks.purpose: Termination of nonlocal maintenance sessions upon completion is an organizational process concern and is not technically verifiable via automated check on Debian

remediation: Implement MFA for all nonlocal maintenance sessions established via external network connections using an approved MFA solution such as PIV cards, FIDO2 tokens, or authenticator apps. Establish and document procedures requiring maintenance personnel to explicitly terminate remote sessions upon completion of maintenance activities. Consider implementing privileged access management tooling to enforce session termination automatically.
